description: Tim Roughgarden discusses the economic implications of providing liquidity in automated market makers (AMMs) like Uniswap, focusing on the balance of costs and benefits for liquidity providers.
topic_summary: The talk explores when it is economically viable to provide liquidity to an AMM, emphasizing the cost of adverse selection and the need for sufficient trading volume to justify participation. Key insights include the calculation of expected losses and the conditions under which liquidity provision is beneficial [t=83:69].
